quranicRelevance: Concealing testimonies or scriptural truths (Kishman - 'yaktumoona ma anzalna'), and hiding thoughts.

keyDerivations.meaning: To hide, conceal, keep back, or suppress

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَكْتُمُونَ مَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ (2:174): They conceal/hide what God revealed (deliberate suppression of scriptural signs).
discardedNoise: Dyeing the hair with a plant named Katam.
Obscure details of sealing clay pots.
summaryEn: The root signifies the concealment of truth, primarily criticizing the active suppression of revelation and testimonies of faith.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على كتمان الحق وإخفائه، مركزاً في القرآن على ذم كتمان الوحي والشهادة لله ولرسوله.
rawText: تَكَاتَمُوا 6 They practised concealment, one with another: see تَدَافَنُوا. تَكَتَّمَ 5 (K, art. دلس) He (a man) concealed, or hid, himself. (T, K, same art.) كَاتِمٌ meaning مَكْتُومٌ: see دَافِقٌ, in two places. === فِى كَاتِمِ السِّرِّ: see a verse cited in conj. 3 of art. عرض. كَتَمَ 1 , aor. ﹹ, inf. n. كَتْمٌ [and كِتْمَانٌ], doubly trans., He concealed, or suppressed, a secret. (Mgh.) === كَتَمَ الرَّبْوَ: see a verse cited in the last paragraph of art. عور. كَتُومٌ A strict concealer of secrets.
